paragraphs: Dr. Cash brings her background in clinical research, human movement, and strength and conditioning into the classroom, helping students connect foundational science with health care practice.
Dr. Cash also mentors students and emerging scholars as they build research skills, connect coursework to clinical questions, and develop confidence in rehabilitation science.
hero.title: Teaching and mentorship

positions.description: Dr. Cash teaches undergraduate anatomy and physiology coursework with an emphasis on clarity, applied learning, and helping students build a strong foundation for future clinical, allied health, and research-oriented work.
positions.role: Adjunct Instructor, Department of General Studies | North Charleston, SC

positions.description: Dr. Cash taught evidence-based practice content for occupational therapy students, supporting students as they developed skills for evaluating research and applying evidence to rehabilitation practice.
positions.role: Adjunct Instructor, Department of Rehabilitation Sciences | Charleston, SC

positions.description: As a guest lecturer, Dr. Cash introduces students to principles of neuromodulation within applied physiology and nutrition, connecting neurophysiological concepts to rehabilitation science and clinical recovery.
positions.role: Guest Lecturer, Department of Rehabilitation Sciences | Charleston, SC

summary.description: Dr. Cash's teaching experience spans anatomy and physiology, evidence-based practice, applied physiology, and neuromodulation. She teaches across undergraduate and graduate health science settings.
summary.title: Classroom and guest instruction
